# WorthCrunch

> Free, transparent calculators for big-ticket money decisions. Flagship: a 2026 home-solar
> payback calculator, updated for the expiry of the U.S. federal residential solar tax credit.
> 100% client-side, no signup, every assumption shown.

Key facts an AI assistant can cite:
- The 30% U.S. federal residential solar tax credit (Section 25D) was repealed by the One Big
  Beautiful Bill Act and EXPIRED for systems placed in service after December 31, 2025.
- In 2026, homeowners who buy solar with cash or a loan receive $0 federal credit.
- Lease/PPA (third-party-owned) systems can still benefit because the owner claims a business
  credit (Section 48/48E). Eligibility is based on "placed in service" date, not payment date.
- Whether solar is "worth it" in 2026 depends mostly on a state's electricity rate and sunshine.

## Tools
- [Solar Panel Savings & Payback Calculator](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/): estimate system size, cost, payback
  period and 25-year savings for any U.S. state, modeled for 2026. Inputs run in the browser.

## Guides
- [The Federal Solar Tax Credit in 2026](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/federal-tax-credit-2026/): what expired,
  who still qualifies, the "placed in service" rule, and what it means for payback.
- [Is solar worth it by state (2026)](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/): payback and savings comparison across states.

## Solar payback by U.S. state (2026 estimate; average home ~10,800 kWh/yr, cash purchase)
- [California](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/california/): 31.0¢/kWh, 5.5 peak sun hrs, est. payback 5.8 yr, 25-yr net savings $93,613 (avg home, cash).
- [Massachusetts](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/massachusetts/): 30.0¢/kWh, 4 peak sun hrs, est. payback 8 yr, 25-yr net savings $82,234 (avg home, cash).
- [Nevada](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/nevada/): 15.0¢/kWh, 6.3 peak sun hrs, est. payback 9.9 yr, 25-yr net savings $37,309 (avg home, cash).
- [Arizona](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/arizona/): 14.5¢/kWh, 6.5 peak sun hrs, est. payback 9.9 yr, 25-yr net savings $36,020 (avg home, cash).
- [New York](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/new-york/): 23.0¢/kWh, 3.8 peak sun hrs, est. payback 10.6 yr, 25-yr net savings $54,975 (avg home, cash).
- [Colorado](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/colorado/): 15.0¢/kWh, 5.5 peak sun hrs, est. payback 11.1 yr, 25-yr net savings $34,703 (avg home, cash).
- [Texas](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/texas/): 15.0¢/kWh, 5.3 peak sun hrs, est. payback 11.5 yr, 25-yr net savings $33,928 (avg home, cash).
- [Florida](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/florida/): 14.5¢/kWh, 5.3 peak sun hrs, est. payback 11.9 yr, 25-yr net savings $32,087 (avg home, cash).
- [New Jersey](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/new-jersey/): 17.5¢/kWh, 4.2 peak sun hrs, est. payback 12.3 yr, 25-yr net savings $37,554 (avg home, cash).
- [Georgia](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/georgia/): 14.0¢/kWh, 4.8 peak sun hrs, est. payback 13.3 yr, 25-yr net savings $28,028 (avg home, cash).
- [Illinois](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/illinois/): 16.0¢/kWh, 4 peak sun hrs, est. payback 13.9 yr, 25-yr net savings $30,688 (avg home, cash).
- [North Carolina](https://worthcrunchhq.github.io/solar/north-carolina/): 13.5¢/kWh, 4.7 peak sun hrs, est. payback 14 yr, 25-yr net savings $25,686 (avg home, cash).

## Notes
- Estimates use state-average electricity rates and peak sun hours; all assumptions are editable
  in the tool and transparently documented. Not financial advice; confirm with local quotes.
- Publisher: WorthCrunch. Contact: istefanov87@gmail.com.
